checks.finding: REQ-006 through REQ-017 define observable files, content, interfaces, diagnostics, compatibility behavior, hash binding, and approval state; AC-001 through AC-016 plus the behavioral and layer contracts provide concrete validation outcomes.

Every goal REQ-006 through REQ-017 is mapped to at least one criterion in the acceptance-test matrix, including cross-cutting parity, LITE exclusion, review-input binding, and approval-boundary requirements.

AC-001 through AC-016 specify inspectable artifact content, stable diagnostics and exits, regression outcomes, manifest hashes, version synchronization, and Draft/Approved state behavior; the behavioral contracts further define inputs and expected results.

The Non-goals section explicitly excludes direct Figma integration, automated visualization, new review stages, sdd-ship and quality-gate changes, Mermaid rendering, and a dedicated backend layer; REQ-013 and workflow boundaries preserve LITE behavior.

Material constraints are explicit: backward-compatible CLI forms and slug validation in REQ-012, Bash/PowerShell parity in REQ-014, canonical hash-bound review inputs in REQ-016, human or signed-sudo approval in REQ-017, and trust/data boundaries in Security Boundaries.

Each stated risk has a planned surface: review-manifest tamper rejection in AC-015, legacy preflight compatibility in AC-011 and TEST-019, LITE and interview burden boundaries in AC-009/AC-013 and TEST-017, and synchronized release metadata in AC-014.
